title: Impeaching Donald John Trump, President of the United States, for high crimes and misdemeanors.
plain_summary: This congressional resolution is a formal legal document that accuses President Donald Trump of seven serious crimes and wrongdoings while in office. The charges include blocking justice in legal cases, illegally controlling money that Congress approved for government spending, misusing trade powers and threatening other countries, punishing people for criticizing him, creating an illegal government office run by Elon Musk, taking bribes and using his position to make money for himself, and trying to act like a dictator by claiming he has more power than the Constitution allows him. These are called articles of impeachment, which is the process Congress uses to formally accuse a president of crimes. If enough members of Congress vote yes, the president could be removed from office.
last_action_text: NOTIFICATION OF INTENT TO OFFER RESOLUTION - Mr. Thanedar notified the House of his intent to offer a privileged resolution pursuant to clause 2(a)(1) of rule IX. The Chair announced that a determinat
subjects: Government employee pay, benefits, personnel management
Government ethics and transparency, public corruption
Presidents and presidential powers, Vice Presidents
Rule of law and government transparency
Sovereignty, recognition, national governance and status

sections.title: Title and Introduction
sections.text: This resolution was introduced in the House of Representatives on April twenty eighth, two thousand twenty five. It was submitted by Representative Thanedar along with Representatives Mfume, Nadler, and Kelly of Illinois. The resolution was sent to the Committee on the Judiciary. The resolution formally accuses Donald John Trump, the President of the United States, of high crimes and misdemeanors and calls for his impeachment.

sections.title: Article I: Obstruction of Justice, Violation of Due Process, and Breach of Duty to Execute Laws
sections.text: The Constitution gives the House of Representatives the sole power to impeach a president. It also says a president can be removed from office if convicted of treason, bribery, or other high crimes and misdemeanors. The Constitution requires the president to make sure laws are carried out faithfully. The Fourth Amendment protects people from unreasonable searches and seizures. The Fifth Amendment says no person can be deprived of life, liberty, or property without due process of law. Donald John Trump violated his oath to faithfully execute the office of president and protect the Constitution. He abused his presidential powers in ways that harm and undermine the Constitution. He used his high office to obstruct justice and corrupt the legal system. He directed his subordinates and attorneys at the Department of Justice to violate their oaths and lie to courts. He sought to dismiss bribery and fraud charges against New York City Mayor Eric Adams in exchange for political cooperation, which is an illegal trade of favors. He fired career Department of Justice attorneys without good reason and in violation of the law because they had investigated him or worked on cases related to the January sixth, two thousand twenty one attack on the Capitol. He directed people to ignore and defy binding court orders. He removed people from the United States to El Salvador despite a court order prohibiting this action, and a court found probable cause that the government committed criminal contempt. He removed Kilmar Armando Abrego Garcia from the United States to El Salvador despite a binding order that prohibited the government from removing him there. He defied court orders, including those upheld by the Supreme Court, to help release Abrego Garcia and provide information to the court and opposing lawyers, while publicly refusing to comply. He acted in contempt of court for violating a court approved settlement agreement. He conducted forced disappearances of people without due process and without telling their families and lawyers where they were, violating the Constitution and Supreme Court rulings. He deliberately and systematically violated laws passed by Congress. He fired Inspector Generals, who are responsible for detecting and preventing waste in government agencies, in violation of the process required by the Inspector Generals Act of nineteen seventy four. He fired a member of the Merit Systems Protection Board, which is a tribunal created to address concerns about federal civil service. He unlawfully shared sensitive personal information of private citizens and government employees, violating federal laws including the Privacy Act and the Administrative Procedure Act. Through all these actions, Donald John Trump willfully ignored federal laws and the Constitution, threatened a co equal branch of government, and endangered the integrity of the democratic system.

sections.title: Article II: Usurpation of the Appropriations Power
sections.text: The Constitution says no money can be taken from the Treasury except through appropriations made by law. This means Congress has the power to decide how government money is spent. Donald John Trump unlawfully took over Congress power to allocate funds, violating the Appropriations Clause in the Constitution and the Impoundment Control Act of nineteen seventy four. Since taking office, he signed many executive orders directing agencies to withhold funds that Congress had appropriated. The president does not have the power to override spending laws or withhold appropriated funds, a principle affirmed by many Supreme Court rulings. He withheld funds and fired many federal employees and shut down numerous federal agencies and departments. He directed the dismantling of the Department of Education, which was established by Congress under the Department of Education Organization Act to reform education, distribute federal aid to students and schools, and prohibit discrimination. The president does not have the authority to dismantle the Department of Education, and his executive order violates the law and Constitution. He unlawfully directed the elimination of the United States Agency for International Development, which plays a crucial role in delivering foreign aid worldwide, including disaster relief, conflict relief, fighting global diseases, promoting education, and improving food security in developing countries. By dismantling this agency and freezing foreign aid, he cut off the United States from its humanitarian role as directed by Congress, leaving other countries and potentially our enemies to fill the role the United States abandoned. He created the so called Department of Government Efficiency, which has stopped payments and taken back funds after they were distributed. Trump used this department to unlawfully block or withhold appropriated funds from dozens of federal agencies, including the National Institutes of Health, the Federal Aviation Administration, and the Department of Veterans Affairs. By withholding congressionally appropriated funds, Trump and this department violated the law and negatively impacted Americans by cutting jobs, stopping critical services, and canceling grants for programs, defying the express will of Congress. He unlawfully withheld congressionally appropriated funds and tried to dismantle congressionally created agencies. By withholding funds and crippling agencies, he prevented the federal government from performing critical duties at home and abroad. He threatened the well being of United States citizens through gross misconduct of his office, resulting in serious and life threatening consequences for the world most vulnerable people.

sections.title: Article III: Abuse of Trade Powers and International Aggression
sections.text: The Constitution gives Congress the power to lay and collect taxes, duties, and excises, and requires that all duties and excises be uniform throughout the United States. Donald John Trump abused trade powers by imposing unjustifiable and unreasonable tariffs on foreign nations, causing a sharp decline in the United States economy and the economies of countries around the world. He declared a false national security emergency to justify his actions. His tariffs contradicted trade agreements established by his administration and other administrations, including binding treaty obligations of the United States, breaking international laws and leading countries around the world to enact damaging tariffs on goods and services from the United States. His Liberation Day scheme imposed ten percent tariffs on all countries, plus additional tariffs based on a formula for reciprocity that was widely mocked as nonsensical. This led to the most significant drop in financial markets since the two thousand twenty crash triggered by the COVID nineteen pandemic. Trump also threatened foreign nations with invasion, annexation of territory, and occupation of sovereign territory through military action. By doing this, he violated constitutionally binding treaty obligations, including the North Atlantic Treaty of nineteen forty nine, the General Treaty for Renunciation of War as an Instrument of National Policy of nineteen twenty eight, the Charter of the United Nations of nineteen forty five, and the Inter American Treaty of Reciprocal Assistance of nineteen forty seven. He threatened the annexation of Canada, stating numerous times that Canada should become the fifty first state under threat of economic coercion and military force. He threatened unlawful and aggressive military action within Mexico, including drone strikes and special operations military personnel against drug cartels, violating Mexican sovereignty without any congressional authorization to use military force. He threatened annexation of Greenland, using hostile language about taking control of Greenland and refusing to rule out military action despite Denmark longstanding alliance with the United States. He threatened annexation of the Panama Canal, stating his administration would be reclaiming the Panama Canal and refusing to rule out military or economic action against the sovereign country. He threatened annexation of the Gaza Strip, involving forced resettlement of the Palestinian population to surrounding countries, stating the United States would take over and own the Gaza Strip. His proposal violates international law, including the Convention on the Prevention and Punishment of the Crime of Genocide, would be unconstitutional without congressional approval, and is profoundly unserious given the grave humanitarian situation in Gaza. Through these actions, he brought shame and embarrassment to the presidency and the American people. He betrayed the trust of the nation to conduct meaningful diplomatic negotiations and failed to maintain peaceful economic and defense relations with foreign countries. He threatened United States security through gross misconduct, prompting hostilities toward Americans and foreign nations warning their citizens against visiting the United States, further harming the nation economy.

sections.title: Article IV: Violation of First Amendment Rights
sections.text: The First Amendment of the Constitution states that Congress cannot make laws that establish religion, prohibit free exercise of religion, limit freedom of speech or press, or restrict the right of people to peacefully assemble and petition the government for addressing their grievances. Donald John Trump retaliated against law firms and attorneys who advised, represented, or were associated with those who represent people who displease him, violating the Constitution prohibition on retaliation against individuals for engaging in First Amendment protected activities. He barred attorneys at certain law firms from accessing federal buildings, arbitrarily revoked security clearances without reasonable basis, threatened to cancel federal contracts with any entity using the targeted attorneys and firms, and demanded free legal services from law firms in exchange for stopping the punitive executive orders. Trump engaged in a pattern of unconstitutional and corrupt retaliation against critics and political opponents, violating First Amendment rights. He weaponized the Department of Justice and other agencies to conduct baseless investigations and harassment of public figures and perceived political enemies. Since taking office, he directed the Department of Justice to dismiss more than a dozen prosecutors who were involved in investigations against him after he left office in two thousand twenty one. The Department of Justice launched investigations into officials involved in prior investigations against Trump, in some cases against individuals specifically named in improper executive orders. He made direct public statements threatening media outlets, current and former officeholders, and advocacy organizations. He pursued meritless lawsuits, used his public office for private gain, and corrupted the justice system to extract tens of millions of dollars in settlements, effectively acting as a conduit for bribery and extortion. He restricted access of the Associated Press after they refused to use the name Gulf of America in their reporting and defied court orders to stop this unconstitutional action. He threatened current and former members of Congress for exercising their legislative and oversight functions, attacking the independence of Congress as established by the Constitution. He repeatedly threatened members of the House Select Committee on the January sixth Attack, which investigated the Capitol attack that Trump instigated, stating they should understand they are subject to investigation and expressing a desire to remove pardons they received. Trump targeted watchdog organizations like Citizens for Responsibility and Ethics in Washington, calling them scum, and independent media outlets like CNN and MSNBC, calling them political arms of the Democratic Party and claiming they are corrupt and illegal. He stated that newspapers critical of him are no different than highly paid political operatives and that their actions should be illegal because they influence judges. Through these actions, he sought to outlaw dissent, opposition, and criticism of himself and his administration. This attack on constitutional rights to freedom of speech, association, and petitioning the government is completely incompatible with the rights and liberties of Americans as a self governing people under the rule of law. He attempted to suppress dissent and opposition, creating a chilling effect on public discourse and striking at core freedoms essential to a free society and democratic elections.

sections.title: Article V: Creation of Unlawful Office
sections.text: The Constitution states that the president shall appoint ambassadors, other public ministers and consuls, judges of the Supreme Court, and all other officers of the United States, but Congress may by law vest the appointment of inferior officers in the president alone, in courts of law, or in heads of departments. Donald John Trump created an unlawful office called the Department of Government Efficiency and gave it extensive unlawful powers, effectively granting this unconstitutional creation significant control over the executive branch. He appointed Elon Musk as the unofficial head of this made up entity, which was not created or funded by any law, without a formal title or office. These actions directly violated the Constitution requirement that principal officers of the United States must be created by law and their appointees confirmed by the Senate, and that inferior offices must be created by law with their appointment process determined by Congress. He gave these powers to Musk without complying with the constitutional requirement that all officers of the United States be commissioned by the president and swear an oath of office to support the Constitution. Musk was designated as a special government employee of the White House Office, violating statutory authorization and restrictions on such designations. This false claim was also used to commit violations of ethics, disclosure, and conflict of interest laws that apply to government officers and employees. Contrary to the government claims in court, Musk exercised actual control over this department and, through it, control over a wide range of government departments and agencies. This reality was repeatedly and openly stated by the Trump administration, including in Trump address to Congress on March fourth, two thousand twenty five. In this capacity, Musk and his subordinates violated the Constitution and laws by withholding funds appropriated by Congress, violating privacy and security laws regarding sensitive information systems and classified material, and firing government employees under false pretenses in violation of civil service laws. These actions vastly exceeded the terms of the formal executive orders that supposedly created this department under the direction of a United States service administrator, an office which remained vacant for several weeks, with the government attorneys claiming in court that the official head was unknown to them. Since then, a nominal administrator was named, despite repeated statements from the president and his administration that Musk controls and directs the department, as confirmed by public reporting and Musk own statements. Trump abused the powers of his office and breached public trust by establishing the so called Department of Government Efficiency and unconstitutionally placing Musk in control of this entity, knowingly providing false testimony to the court regarding the leadership of the entity, and directing the unlawful entity to implement his agenda, violating the Constitution objective of establishing justice and fundamental rules for how the executive branch is structured and how people may be appointed to public office.

sections.title: Article VI: Bribery and Corruption
sections.text: The Constitution states that no person holding any office of profit or trust under the United States shall, without the consent of Congress, accept any present, payment, office, or title of any kind from any foreign state. Donald John Trump engaged in a pattern of unlawful corruption for his personal profit and that of his associates, soliciting and accepting bribes in exchange for official actions, policy influence, and favorable treatment from his administration. He profited from public office on a scale unprecedented in American history. Trump engaged in fraudulent schemes through pump and dump or rug pull tactics for cryptocurrency tokens he created, while also enabling a direct way for bribes to be paid to him in exchange for official actions. He used the threat of hostile government action and the offer of preferential treatment to extract large payments in the form of settlements for meritless lawsuits he pursues personally, thereby corrupting both the executive branch and the judiciary. To advance this goal, Trump solicited and received nine hundred forty million dollars in free legal services from prominent law firms to help causes he personally supports. He refused to divest himself or take any steps to prevent conflicts of interest in his substantial personal business ventures, creating another way for bribes to be paid. He permitted and encouraged his subordinates to violate laws regarding ethics, disclosures, and self dealing, and through these actions enabled and encouraged corruption by his relatives, associates, and political allies. Through these and other ways, he solicited payments from foreign governments for corrupt purposes and in clear violation of the Constitution prohibition on foreign payments.

sections.title: Article VII: Tyranny
sections.text: The Constitution establishes a system of checks and balances and separation of powers to ensure that the office of the president does not become tyrannical. Donald John Trump sought to establish himself as a tyrant, dictator, and autocrat over the people of the United States, taking for himself the constitutional powers of Congress, the courts, and the states, as well as powers that are illegitimate and beyond the scope of lawful government. He claimed for himself absolute and arbitrary power, the ability to suspend laws at will, and completely disregarded and betrayed his oath to faithfully execute the office of president and to preserve, protect, and defend the Constitution. He denied and violated the right of Americans to freedom of speech, assembly, and to petition the government for addressing their grievances. He denied and violated the right of Americans to due process of law and equal protection under the laws, to fair and impartial independent courts, and to legal counsel. He sought to intimidate and coerce representatives and senators in Congress through means including the threat of unlawful prosecutions and by encouraging violence and threats of violence. He denied and violated the separation of powers and the Constitution system of checks and balances, taking for himself the legislative and judicial powers, including the power of Congress to levy taxes, appropriate public funds, direct the organization of officers and agencies of the executive branch, and to make laws. He sought to intimidate, coerce, and extort the officers of the states, including those elected by their people, and thereby sought to commandeer the states, violating their sovereignty and the system of federalism established by the Constitution. He sought to eliminate the citizenship clause of the Fourteenth Amendment by issuing an executive order that directs executive departments and agencies to withhold citizenship from certain classes of people who are born within the United States. He repeatedly threatened and suggested that he intends to violate the presidential term limits established by the Twenty second Amendment and adopted openly monarchical aspirations, including by invoking theories that he is above the law or that his personal will constitutes the law. Through all of this, Trump willfully disregarded federal laws and the Constitution, threatened a co equal branch of government, and endangered the integrity of the democratic system. He betrayed his trust as president, abused the powers of the presidency, acted in a manner completely incompatible with self governance and the rule of law, and committed high crimes and misdemeanors in attempting to establish himself as a lawless tyrant to the clear injury of the American people.
